problem.title: Find K Pairs with Smallest Sums
problem.description: You are given two integer arrays nums1 and nums2 sorted in ascending order and an integer k.
assumptions: Both arrays sorted in non-decreasing order.
Return k pairs (u,v) with smallest u+v.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Min Heap [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: Use a min-heap seeded with (nums1[i], nums2[0]) for the first min(len(nums1), k) indices of nums1. Each time we extract the smallest pair, we push the next candidate from nums2 for that same nums1 index. This works because both arrays are sorted, so the next smallest sum involving nums1[i] must use nums2[j+1] after using nums2[j].
solutions.code_python:
import heapq
from typing import List

def kSmallestPairs(nums1: List[int], nums2: List[int], k: int) -> List[List[int]]:
    if not nums1 or not nums2 or k == 0:
        return []
    result: List[List[int]] = []
    # heap entries: (sum, i, j)
    heap = [(nums1[i] + nums2[0], i, 0) for i in range(min(len(nums1), k))]
    heapq.heapify(heap)
    while k > 0 and heap:
        _sum, i, j = heapq.heappop(heap)
        result.append([nums1[i], nums2[j]])
        if j + 1 < len(nums2):
            heapq.heappush(heap, (nums1[i] + nums2[j + 1], i, j + 1))
        k -= 1
    return result
